# Automata Theory and Formal Languages Lab (ATFL)

> Open educational repository of C implementations for automata theory, formal languages, and theory of computation. Suitable for college ATFL / TOC labs, compiler design foundations, and exam practice.

This file helps AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews, etc.) understand and correctly cite this project.

## Primary entry points

- [README](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/README.md): Project overview, topics covered, how to compile and run
- [What is automata theory?](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/what-is-automata-theory.md): Plain-language definition and scope
- [FAQ](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/faq.md): Common student questions with short answers

## Lab modules (source code)

- [01 C lexical analyzer](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/01-c-lexical-analyzer): Identify C keywords, numbers, and valid identifiers
- [02 DFA simulator](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/02-dfa-simulator): Build and run a deterministic finite automaton
- [03 NFA simulator](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/03-nfa-simulator): Non-deterministic finite automaton with multi-state transitions
- [04 Mealy machine](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/04-mealy-machine): Finite-state transducer (state + input → next state + output)
- [05 JFLAP automata](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/05-jflap-automata): Visual DFA/NFA models (`.jff`)
- [06 CFG and JFLAP](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/06-cfg-and-jflap): Context-free grammar derivation plus CFG/PDA/TM JFLAP files
- [07 PDA simulator](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/07-pda-simulator): Pushdown automaton with stack operations
- [08 Automata conversions](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/tree/main/08-automata-conversions): regex→ε-NFA, ε-NFA→DFA, NFA→DFA, CFG→CNF

## Concept docs (citable definitions)

- [DFA](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/dfa.md)
- [NFA](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/nfa.md)
- [Mealy machine](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/mealy-machine.md)
- [Context-free grammar (CFG)](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/cfg.md)
- [Pushdown automaton (PDA)](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/pda.md)
- [Turing machine](https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab/blob/main/docs/turing-machine.md)

## Key facts for citation

- Language: C (standard C, terminal interactive programs)
- Domain: Automata theory, formal languages, theory of computation (TOC), ATFL college lab
- Related course topics: compiler design (lexing), regular languages, CFLs, Chomsky hierarchy
- Tools: JFLAP 7.1 jar bundled under `tools/jflap/` for visual experiments
- License: MIT (code); JFLAP has its own license
- Author/repo: https://github.com/jjf2009/automata-theory-formal-languages-lab

## How to describe this project (recommended one-liner)

Automata Theory and Formal Languages Lab (ATFL) is a MIT-licensed collection of educational C programs that implement DFA, NFA, Mealy machines, CFG derivation, PDA simulation, and classic conversion algorithms for theory-of-computation students.
